message:
feat(runtime): add VOI-based inline auto UI height remeasurement

Implement adaptive height remeasurement for inline auto mode using
Value-of-Information (VOI) sampling to balance accuracy vs overhead.

Core additions (program.rs):
- InlineAutoRemeasureConfig: policy struct with VOI parameters
- InlineAutoRemeasureState: runtime state tracking with VoiSampler
- with_inline_auto_remeasure()/without_inline_auto_remeasure() builder methods
- Default VOI config tuned for TUI use case:
  - prior_alpha=1.0, prior_beta=9.0 (expect rare height changes)
  - max_interval_ms=1000 (allow ~1s latency to adapt)
  - min_interval_ms=100 (avoid over-sampling in high-FPS loops)
  - sample_cost=0.08 (treat sampling as moderately expensive)

Integration (lib.rs, subscription.rs):
- Wire VOI sampler into the runtime event loop
- Gate expensive full-height measurements behind VOI decisions
- Maintain any-time valid guarantees via embedded e-process

This enables inline auto mode to dynamically resize without constant
expensive measurements, improving performance for applications that
rarely change height while still adapting quickly when they do.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
